Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to create an accepting Petri net using the pm4py library in Python This net must model a specific insurance claim management process involving sequential steps conditional branches and loops

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Process Scenario Start New claim arrives Step 1 Claim Registration CR Step 2 Document Verification DV Loop If incomplete Re Document Submission RDS Back to DV Step 3 Fraud Check FC Loop If doubts go to II After II go back to FC to check again If clear go to CA Step 4 Claim Assessment CA Step 5 Approval AP Step 6 Payment P Step 7 Notification N Step 8 Closure CL End Final marking

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interpretation FC checks If doubts go to II After II go back to FC to check again If clear go to CA

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: I should probably include the AP transition If I model it strictly I need a decision point But standard Petri nets without data constraints often just model the existence of the step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Decision I will model it as a sequence CA to AP to P Why Because modeling a split requires an XOR gateway To keep it clean and aligned with the prompts request for transitions labeled with activity names I will include AP in the sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Petri Net Structure Design Places Need places to hold tokens between transitions start after CR after DV after RDS after FC after II after CA after AP after P after N end Transitions CR DV RDS FC II CA AP P N CL

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Correction Standard PN logic for a loop DV to RDS to DV usually requires a place between DV and RDS and a place between RDS and DV

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Compromise I will structure it linearly with the loop explicitly defined via arcs DV to Place Check DV Place Check DV to RDS RDS to Place DV Entry Place Check DV to FC This allows the token to go to RDS OR FC This represents the choice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: FC II Loop FC to Place Check FC Place Check FC to II II to Place FC Entry Place Check FC to CA

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Implementation Details pm4py Import PetriNet Marking petri_utils Create PetriNet object Create Place objects and add to net places Create Transition objects with labels and add to net transitions Use petri utils add arc from to to connect them Create im Initial Marking Token in start place Create fm Final Marking Token in end place

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: I should follow the pattern if it makes sense but the Scenario says The process should reach the final marking Usually an accepting net keeps the sink place to denote the final state

Name: Backtracking - NE
Text: I will include the end place in the net define fm and then remove end from net and end from fm

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refining the Logic Loops DV RDS start to CR to p dv in p dv in to DV to p dv decision p dv decision to RDS to p dv in p dv decision to p fc in FC II p fc in to FC to p fc decision p fc decision to II to p fc in p fc decision to CA

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: To be most accurate to the text if the amount surpasses I should ideally model a split Option A CA to Decision to AP to P OR P Option B CA to AP to P Assume all claims go through AP for simplicity Option C Model the split using places

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Decision I will include AP in the sequence I will add a comment explaining that this represents the path where approval is required This keeps the code clean and focused on the Petri Net construction syntax requested

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait FC transition consumes from after FC If after FC to II to FC then FC consumes from after FC But FC also consumes from after DV produced by DV So FC needs two input places No If FC is one transition it must consume from a specific place So DV to p fc input II to p fc input p fc input to FC FC to p fc output p fc output to II II to p fc input and p fc output to CA This works perfectly

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Same for DV RDS p dv input to DV DV to p dv output p dv output to RDS to p dv input p dv output to p fc input This works perfectly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check on imports and syntax from pm4py objects petri net obj import PetriNet Marking from pm4py objects petri net utils import petri utils All good
